story_shape: the captain who came home -- a veteran interior anchor returns for a fifth year and a draft-stock final ride

plan.spine: a 300-pound team captain returns for a fifth season to anchor a BYU defense he believes is on the verge of something special
blend: 2025: 26 tackles, 2.0 sacks in his first BYU season (Utah transfer)
6-4, 300; team captain and defensive line leader
returned for a fifth year after his coach stayed at BYU
frames 2026 as his draft-stock final season
plan.payoff: a steady debut BYU year as the line's veteran leader
plan.forward_stake: whether the veteran anchor lifts a BYU defense into the CFP conversation

hook: Keanu Tanuvasa is a captain, a 300-pound anchor, and a veteran who chose to come back for a fifth year -- because he believes this BYU defense is on the verge of something special, and he wants one more shot at it.
expand: The Utah transfer settled in as BYU's defensive-line leader in 2025 -- 26 tackles, two sacks, and the steady interior presence a defense organizes around -- and was named a team captain. He returned for a fifth season largely because head coach Kalani Sitake stayed, framing 2026 openly as his draft-stock final ride. The forward stakes are a team's ceiling: a veteran anchor in the middle is the unglamorous foundation of good run defense, and Tanuvasa believes the unit around him is ready to break through. He's not a stat-sheet stuffer -- he's the captain who holds the point and sets the tone for a defense with real ambitions.
style: Tanuvasa is a classic anchor tackle -- 6-4, 300, built to occupy blockers and hold the interior against the run. The value isn't in flashy numbers (his sack and tackle totals are modest); it's in the dirty work and the leadership, the captain who two-gaps, eats double-teams, and frees the players around him to make plays. He's a veteran's veteran, a former missionary and fifth-year leader whose game is about reliability and toughness in the middle. On a defense aiming to take a step, his job is the foundation everything else is built on.
style_layers_pending: no offensive PBP for a defensive tackle -- box (tackles/sacks) + role/leadership grounded; fan-vs-rival discourse descriptors pending discourse-extraction cleanup
